message:
fix(runtime): atomic saturating_decrement and polls_remaining consumption

GlobalInjector::saturating_decrement() — Replaced fetch_sub + store-on-
underflow with fetch_update(checked_sub).  The previous approach had a
brief window where the counter would wrap to usize::MAX before the
corrective store, confusing len(), is_empty(), and has_timed_work()
readers.  fetch_update is slightly more expensive in the common case
(CAS loop vs single fetch_sub) but eliminates the transient-underflow
observation window entirely.

StoredTask + LocalStoredTask — polls_remaining is now consumed via
.take() at the start of poll(), before the trace! invocation, instead
of being read via unwrap_or(0) without consumption.  This means:
  1. The budget_remaining variable reflects the actual budget at poll
     entry rather than a stale value that survives across polls.
  2. The leading underscore on _budget_remaining (which suppressed the
     unused-variable lint) is no longer needed.

Added two tests verifying that polls_remaining is None after a single
poll for both StoredTask and LocalStoredTask.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
